subject: Product Management
title: The World Wide Web
description: A fast breakdown of The World Wide Web for builders.

hook.text: Tim Berners-Lee didn't just invent a technology. He made a product decision that changed everything: he gave it away free.

buildup.text: In 1989, Berners-Lee proposed a system for sharing documents at CERN using hyperlinks, URLs, and HTML.

discovery.text: His boss wrote on the proposal: 'Vague but exciting.' Berners-Lee built it anyway — browser, server, and protocol.

twist.text: CERN could have patented and licensed the web. Instead, Berners-Lee insisted it be free and open to everyone.

climax.text: That one product decision — free and open — enabled every website, app, and online service that followed.

punchline.text: The most valuable product in history was given away for free.

quiz.question: What product decision made the World Wide Web transformative?
options: Making it free and open instead of patented and licensed
Creating the first social media platform
